other_uses: Low plains yucca with narrow blue-green leaves and greenish-white flower spikes; the hardiest yucca.
prep: Cook the flowers (petals, de-bittered) and the green fruit as vegetables; the leaf fibers make cordage and the roots a soap ('amole'). An extremely cold- and drought-hardy Great Plains plant, tied to its yucca moth.
harvest: Flowers early summer; fruit late summer.
soil: Dry, sandy or rocky, well-drained soils.
risks: Leaf tips sharp; roots soapy (not eaten).
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
